_notes: Per-rack pallet counts, bay counts, and level counts come from data/rack-drawings/*.pdf
(PDF stamp grand-total 3203 — but real per-bay widths differ; see May 11 update).
Rearranged May 6 from the team's hand-drawn top-down sketch:
- Vertical racks ordered along the building. U is a single horizontal rack at
the north end (y=37). J is a polyline bracket; A is the easternmost peer in
the line of single-rack columns.
- J's bottom arm meets G at a CLOSED junction (no traffic).
- J's top arm meets U at a CLOSED junction (no traffic).
Coordinates are approximate until May 20 site measurement; topology is committed.
kit_corridor_side / rt_aisle_side preserved from the previously confirmed B-G
alternation; J and H's adjacent corridor types are TBD until measurement.
May 6 (later correction from feedback on 3D render):
- H, I raised so south ends are above J's bottom arm (y=11) — they live INSIDE
J's bracket but do not pass through the bottom arm.
- G is the only rack that overlaps J's bottom arm (its body passes through y=10
and meets J at the closed junction). G + F-B north ends shortened to y=36.35
so they don't intersect U's horizontal at y=37.
- U east end pulled back so U doesn't extend past A. 14 bays at ~1.73 m each.
May 11 (per-bay widths from WhatsApp + cross-checked against SAP özet bin codes):
- `pallets_per_bay` on a segment is the DEFAULT width; `bay_overrides` lists
bays that deviate. Width = pallet positions across the bay (1, 2, or 3).
- Reduced widths (1 or 2) are feeder bays where material size determines
how many pallets physically fit. Confirmed in SAP: max position used per
bay equals the WhatsApp width on every reduced bay.
- J12 has width 0 (reserved for a parked cart per site report).
- U uses position_offset=0 (positions 1, 2) on all bays EXCEPT U8, which uses
position_offset=1 (positions 2, 3) — that's what 'U 8 hariç, 1 yok' meant.
- U14 is a KÜÇÜK RF small rack with non-standard addressing (SAP shows pos
3, 5, 6); modelled as width 2 for now and flagged for May 20.
May 11 (CAD-driven integration — this commit):
- X-AXIS FLIPPED. CAD shows J at the bottom-right of the floor plan; our
previous coords placed J at the far west. Every horizontal coordinate is
now mirrored around x = building.width_m / 2 = 40. Sides flipped
east<->west; vertical sides (north/south) unchanged. After the flip:
J's main vertical sits at x≈70 (east wall), U east end at x=59.2,
A at x≈36, kitting at x=60-72 (south-east corner).
- J 5-SECTION POLYLINE per J.pdf. The PDF draws J as five detached
sub-sections in elevation: [J1] [J2-J12] [J13 KÜÇÜK RF] [J14] [J15-J18].
Our previous 4+10+4 model split was wrong — the actual segmentation
is 1+11+1+1+4. J13 and J14 are detached single bays; J13 is the small
KÜÇÜK RF rack flagged in the PDF.
- B SPLIT per B.pdf. The PDF draws B as two visually separated sections,
B1-B6 and B8-B12, with B7 absent. Modelled here as two segments; B7 is
omitted entirely. Capacity drops by 27 pallet positions (3 × 9 levels)
versus the May 6 single-segment B.
- U14 DETACHED per U.pdf. U.pdf draws U14 as a stand-alone KÜÇÜK RF small
rack at the west end of U; main run is U1-U13.
- PRODUCTION-LINE map (ASSUMPTIONS §17) — provisional wires: GAM along J's
main vertical (no side committed yet, kit_corridor_side stays TBD until
May 20). Other 5 lines documented in production_lines below; exact
corridor assignment TBD on the May 20 site visit.
- CLOSED JUNCTIONS list added so the renderer can draw the J↔G and J↔U
closed-junction X marks the CAD shows.

racks._geometry: 5-section polyline per J.pdf: [J1] south stub + [J2-J12] main vertical + [J13 KÜÇÜK RF] detached + [J14] detached + [J15-J18] top arm. Post May-11 x-flip: main vertical at x≈70 (east wall), top arm runs west toward U at closed junction (59.2, 37), bottom J1 stub sits at closed junction with G (59.2, 10), J13 and J14 sit detached east of the main run.

segments._label: J1 bottom arm (1 bay) — at G closed junction
J2-J12 main vertical (11 bays) — east wall
J13 KÜÇÜK RF — detached small rack per J.pdf
J14 single bay — detached per J.pdf
J15-J18 top arm (4 bays) — at U closed junction

racks._orientation: vertical, lives INSIDE J's bracket — south end y=11 stays north of J's bottom arm (y=10), north end y=36.35 stays south of U (y=37). Post-flip: H sits between J's main vertical (x=70) and I (x=62.4).
vertical, INSIDE J's bracket like H — south above J bottom arm, north below U. Post-flip: I↔G is the kit corridor (matches G's kit_east).
vertical, the ONLY rack whose body overlaps J's bottom arm — passes through y=10 and meets J1 at CLOSED junction near (59.2, 10). North end y=36.35 stays south of U. Post-flip x=59.1.
vertical, north end y=36.35 stays south of U. Post-flip x=54.8.
vertical, north end y=36.35 stays south of U. Post-flip x=51.5.
vertical, north end y=36.35 stays south of U. Post-flip x=47.2.
vertical, north end y=36.35 stays south of U. Post-flip x=43.9.

vertical, but split into two physical sections per B.pdf: B1-B6 (south) and B8-B12 (north). B7 is absent (physical gap, not just an ÖN/ARKA cutout). North end y=36.35 stays south of U. Post-flip x=39.6.

segments._label: B8-B12 north section (B7 absent per B.pdf)

racks._orientation: vertical, post-flip leftmost (west) of the single-rack column line; 11 bays A2-A12 per A.pdf. Post-flip x=36.3.

horizontal (E-W) at north end y=37, split into main run (U1-U13) and detached U14 KÜÇÜK RF per U.pdf. Post-flip: U1 at east end (x=59.2) meets J's top arm at CLOSED junction; U13 at x≈36.7; U14 KÜÇÜK RF detached at the far west (x≈33.5-35.5). Bay width ~1.73m (PDF didn't stamp). Per May 11 SAP cross-check, U bays are 2 pallets wide (positions 1, 2); bay 8 is the exception (positions 2, 3 — 'U8 1 yok').

segments._label: U14 KÜÇÜK RF — detached small rack per U.pdf
